2604.21794.title: Learning to Communicate: Toward End-to-End Optimization of Multi-Agent Language Systems
2604.21794.paper_summary: Multi-agent systems built on large language models have shown strong performance on complex reasoning tasks, yet most work focuses on agent roles and orchestration while treating inter-agent communication as a fixed interface. Latent communication through internal representations such as key-value caches offers a promising alternative to text-based protocols, but existing approaches do not jointly optimize communication with multi-agent reasoning. Therefore we propose DiffMAS, a training framework that treats latent communication as a learnable component of multi-agent systems. DiffMAS performs parameter-efficient supervised training over multi-agent latent trajectories, enabling agents to jointly learn how information should be encoded and interpreted across interactions. Experiments on mathematical reasoning, scientific QA, code generation, and commonsense benchmarks show that DiffMAS consistently improves reasoning accuracy and decoding stability over single-agent inference, text-based multi-agent systems, and prior latent communication methods, achieving 26.7% on AIME24, 20.2% on GPQA-Diamond, and consistent gains across reasoning benchmarks.
2604.21794.paper_summary_zh: 多代理系統基於大型語言模型在複雜推理任務上表現出色，然而大多數工作專注於代理角色和協調，同時將代理間通信視為固定接口。通過內部表示（如鍵值緩存）進行潛在通信提供了一種有前景的替代文本協議的方法，但現有的方法並未共同優化通信與多代理推理。因此，我們提出了DiffMAS，一個將潛在通信視為多代理系統可學習組件的訓練框架。DiffMAS在多代理潛在軌跡上進行參數高效的監督訓練，使代理能夠共同學習如何在互動中編碼和解釋信息。在數學推理、科學問答、代碼生成和常識基準上的實驗表明，DiffMAS在推理準確性和解碼穩定性上始終優於單代理推理、基於文本的多代理系統和先前的潛在通信方法，在AIME24上達到26.7%，在GPQA-Diamond上達到20.2%，並在推理基準上持續獲得增益。
2604.21794.author: Ye Yu et.al.
2604.21794.authors: Ye Yu, Heming Liu, Haibo Jin, Xiaopeng Yuan, Peng Kuang, Haohan Wang
